title: ramium hispidulum
intro: Ramium hispidulum, also known as the prickly ash or toothache tree, is a plant species that belongs to the Rutaceae family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, making it a potential treatment for toothaches, arthritis, and other painful conditions. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its high concentration of alkaloids, such as berberine and palmitic acid, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. Ramium hispidulum can be prepared as a tincture or infusion, and it is often used in herbal remedies to relieve pain and reduce inflammation, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
